California
Fair Political
SUPERSEDED
Practices Cornmission
IN PART
August 
■3′  ■99■
b3 A-i.t -811 (crniny
Arnold M. clasman
The city of Pomona
city HalIr 505 So. Garey Avenue
Box 660
Pomona, cA 9L'| 69
Re: Your Request for Advice
our File No. A-91-3 4 5
Dear Mr. Glaslnan:
You have requested advice on behalf of the city of Pomona
Vice Mayor NelI Soto and Councilmember Tomas Ursua concerning the
con f l- ict -of- interest provisions of the PoliticaL Reform Act (the
rra6grr 1 . 1
Since our advj.ce is lj-mited only to provisions of the Act, we
make no comnent regarding any other conf l ict-o f - interest Iaws.
Furthermore, our advice is prospective in nature and we do not
comment or advise regarding past conduct.
OUESTION
vice Mayor Soto and councilmenber Ursua are named defendants
in a lawsuit. 
Under the Act, rnay they participate in a
governmental decj.sion regarding their requests for the city to
provide then with a legal defense?
CONCLUS ION
Vice Mayor Soto and councilnenber Ursua rnay participate in a
decisj,on regarding their requests for the city to provide them
with a legal defense as Long as payment of attorneys' fees is
considered either conpensation or terms and conditions of offi'ce.
ceneraLly, lrhen a governmental entity pays for the attorneys' fees
1' 
Governnent code Sections 81000-91015. All statutory references
are to the covernment code unless otherwise indicated. 
Commission
regulations appear at 2 california code of Regulations Section
18OOO, et seq. AIl references to regulations are to Title 2,
Division 6 of the Californj.a Code of Regulations.
428 J Street, Suite 8OO . P.O. Box 8O7 o Sacramento CA 958O4.O8O7 o (916) 122-5660


Our Fi■e No. A-9■-345
Page 2
of its officers or employees for acts wj.thin the scope of their
employment, the payment is in the nature of compensation if the
payment of the attorneys' fees is authorized by law.
rACTS
A civil conplaint, filed in the Superior court of Los Angeles
county, named Tomas Ursua and NeLl Soto as defendants and alleged
that they made disparaging rernarks which caused the plaintiffs
injury. 
These remarks revolve around a report, prepared by the
f irrn of saranow, WeIIs & Enirhanian, which was authorized by the
city in order to investigate alteged irnproprieties by forner city
staff. 
This confidentiaL report was distributed to the city
council. A private citizen obtained possession of the report and
released it to the general public and the }ocal newspapers.
The cornplaint alleges that councilmember Ursua made
statements to reporters from the Inland vallev Dailv Bul-Ietin and
the Los Anqeles Times concerning the report. Mr. Ursua and other
councilmembers were contacted by nevspaper reporters who were
preparing a story concerning the issue.
The conplaint further alleges that Vice Mayor soto nade
several statenents at a candidates' forum. which was sponsored by
the Pomona Chamber of Conmerce and held in conjunction with the
campaign for the April, 1991 city council- election. vice Mayor
Soto, who was introduced as the city's vice mayor and referred to
by her office during the forun, is alleged to have made several
statenents alluding to the investigation and the report.
The issue of the city providing a legal defense was discussed
and voted upon at the June 11, 1991 city council neeting. The
council deter:nined that both vice Mayor soto and councilmember
Ursua were acting outside the course and scope of their ernployment
and voted against providing a defense in this matter. UPon your
recomnendation, n"lther Ms. soto nor Mr- ursua participated in the
closed session discussion or the vote concerning their defense'
Both councilnenbers have requested that the FPPC evaluate the j'r
ability to now participate in this decision-
ANALYS I S
Section 8710o prohibits PubIic officials 
from naking,
participating in, oi using their official 
position to. infLuence a
fovernrnlnta I 
- decis ion in which they have a financial. interest. 
An
5rfi.iut 
has a financia] interest in a decision within the meaning
of Section 87100 j.f it is reasonably foreseeable that the decision
*iIt hur" a material financial effe-t, distinguishable frorn its
"ii."[-"" 
the public genera]Iy, on the official, 
on a member of
the official's 
immediate family, or on:
(a) Any business entity in which the public
officiai 
has a direct or indirect investment worth
one thousand dollars (51.000) or more'


Our Fi■e NO. A-9■-345
Page 3
(b) Any real Property in which the public
official 
has a direct or indirect interest worth
one thousand dollars (S1,000) or more.
(c) Any source of incone, other than gifts
and other than loans by a commercial lending
institution in the regular course of business on
terms available to the public without regard to
officiat 
status, aggregating tuo hundred fifty
dollars ($250) or roore in value provided to,
received by or pronised to the public official
within 12 months prior to the time when the
decision is made.
(d) Any business entity in which the public
official 
is a director, officer, partner, trustee,
empLoyee, or holds any position of management.
(e) Any donor of, or any intermediary or
agent for a donor of, a gift or gifts aggregating
two hundred fifty dollars (5250) or more in value
provided to, received by, or prornised to the public
official 
within 12 months prior to the time when
the decision is nade.
Section 87103 .
The effect of a governnental decision is material- if the
decision wilI resuLt in the personal expenses, income, assets
(other than interest in real property), or J.iabilities of the
officiaL or his or her inmediate fanily increasing or decreasing
by at least 9250. (Regulation la7o2- 1(a) (4).) 
For Purposes of
this analysis, we wiII assume that the cost of each
councilnember's legal representation in this natter r'rilI exceed
$250. Accordingly, the effect of a decision to provide a legal
defense would be considered material i'n that it wilI increase or
decrease the assets or Iiabilities 
of each councilnenber by $250
or nore.
The next deterrnination is whether participating in a deci-sion
to provide the councilnenbers with a legal defense constitutes the
rnaking of a governnental decision. Regulati'on 18700(d) provides
that making or participating in the making of a governmental
decision shall not include:
Actions by public officials, 
employees, or
enployee representatives relating to their
cornpensation or the terms or conditions of their
emplo)ment or contract.
Regulation 18700(d)(3)

concLusion with respect to the other councilmember. Therefore, it
is reasonably foreseeable that any decision regarding the lega]
defense wilI affect the financial interests of both
councilnembers. Accordingly, a disqualified councilmember may not
participate in such decisions unless his or her participation is
1egal1y required to obtain a quorun.
